prompts.text: Resolve Regeneration for any eligible units before moving
prompts.detail: Regeneration is declared at the start of the Movement phase, before any orders are issued.

prompts.text: Issue Command Orders before units move
prompts.detail: Commands can be issued at any point during a unit's move, but declaring them first avoids forgetting.

prompts.text: Are vulnerable units protected? Check chaff unit angles
prompts.detail: Angle chaff units in front of valuable shooting units to prevent enemy charges fitting in gaps.

prompts.text: Pivoting: you can pivot through units — but must end clear
prompts.detail: Units can pivot through friendly and enemy units and all terrain types, but the entire move must end clear.

prompts.text: Charging units treat all friendlies as Blocking Terrain
prompts.detail: You cannot charge through friendly units. Exception: other units simultaneously charging the same target.

prompts.text: Wild Charge units: measure first, declare, then roll
prompts.detail: Measure to target first. If in range of [Sp×2 + max Wild Charge], declare the charge, then roll. If roll fails, give a different order — you cannot select a new charge target.

prompts.text: Check every unit — heroes, spellcasters, ranged weapons
prompts.detail: Individual heroes, spellcasters, and units with ranged special rules are easy to forget. Go through the full roster.

prompts.text: Cover check: -1 to hit unless height difference is 3+ levels
prompts.detail: Target is in cover if you cannot draw a line to at least half its facing without obstruction, or its centre-point is in Difficult Terrain. Ignore if intervening unit/terrain is 3+ height levels smaller than both shooter and target.

prompts.text: Ranged attacks cause Wavering only — not Rout (unless target is Devastated)
prompts.detail: A failed Nerve test from ranged fire results in Wavering, not removal. Exception: Devastated units treat Wavering as Rout.

prompts.text: Shooting from within Difficult Terrain: no penalty to the shooter
prompts.detail: A unit with its centre-point inside Difficult Terrain can ignore that terrain when determining its own LOS.

prompts.text: Hindered charge? -1 to hit for this combat
prompts.detail: A charge is Hindered if the charging unit's move went through or ended over any Difficult Terrain or Obstacle. Units ordered to Reform are never Hindered.

prompts.text: Any units with Brutal? Apply modifier to damage rolls
prompts.detail: Brutal adds to the damage roll result. Multiple sources of Brutal are cumulative.

prompts.text: Elite or Vicious? Re-roll misses / re-roll 1s to damage
prompts.detail: Elite: re-roll all failed to-hit dice. Vicious: re-roll all dice that score a natural unmodified 1 when rolling to damage.

prompts.text: Iron Resolve? If Steady, regain (n) damage points (max 3)
prompts.detail: If the unit passes its Nerve test (is Steady), it regains n points of previously suffered damage, up to a maximum of 3.

prompts.text: Check magic items on this unit before rolling
prompts.detail: Review artefacts on the attacking unit. Apply any combat modifiers before rolling to hit.

prompts.text: Know your Defence stat when being damaged
prompts.detail: Your opponent needs to match or beat your De value when rolling to damage. Know it before they roll.

prompts.text: Know your Nerve stat — check if you're at risk before the roll
prompts.detail: Add current damage to the 2D6 roll. If the total meets or exceeds your Ne value, you fail.

prompts.text: Any of your units Wavering? They can only Halt!, Change Facing!, or Back!
prompts.detail: Wavering units cannot charge, shoot, or use most orders. Wavering is removed at the end of the unit's own next Turn.

prompts.text: Inspiring nearby? Failed Nerve test must be re-rolled
prompts.detail: If a friendly Inspiring unit is within 6" (or 9" for Very Inspiring), re-roll the failed Nerve test. Second result stands.

quick_reference.title: Withdrawing
quick_reference.body: Take a Nerve test first. Pass: face enemy, move 2D6" directly backward — terrain does not affect this movement. Touch an enemy unit = Routed. Touch Blocking Terrain = stop and Wavering. Can move through friendlies; if you cannot clear them, keep going until clear then Wavering.

quick_reference.title: Devastated units
quick_reference.body: When damage exceeds the unit's Nerve value, it is Devastated. Halve Attacks (round down). Any failed Nerve test now causes Rout — no more Wavering results.

quick_reference.title: We Are Doomed (double 6)
quick_reference.body: Ranged: unit Wavers even if it would have passed. Combat or Withdraw: unit takes D6 extra damage (no further Nerve test triggered). Double 1 always passes regardless of damage.

quick_reference.title: Flank and rear charges
quick_reference.body: Attacks directed at a flank are doubled before rolling to hit. Attacks directed at a rear are tripled. Determined by where the charging unit's front facing mid-point sits at time of declaration.
